task_completion.description: Open the Copper Point of Sale application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Copper Point of Sale application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Import the inventory items from the CSV file into Copper POS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Copper POS Items list shows the imported items (e.g., Coffee Beans 1lb, Trail Mix Bag, USB-C Cable 6ft).

task_completion.description: Create the 4 required categories in Copper POS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A categories list, dropdown, or management window in Copper POS showing 'Beverages', 'Snacks', 'Electronics', and 'Stationery'.

task_completion.description: Assign the imported items to their respective categories in Copper POS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Item properties, edit windows, or the main item list in Copper POS showing items associated with the correct categories.

task_completion.description: Create the category_assignments.txt summary file with the correct format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like Notepad) or terminal window showing the creation of 'category_assignments.txt' with lines formatted exactly as 'Item Name | Category Name' for the 12 items.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the Copper POS GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with the Copper POS interface, such as clicking through menus, import dialogs, or item editing windows, rather than just terminal windows.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the POS application by only writing a script to generate the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Copper POS must be shown with the imported and categorized items. The agent should not just use PowerShell/Python to read the CSV and write the TXT file without touching the POS.

integrity.description: The summary file is saved in the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor's title bar, Save As dialog, or terminal path should indicate the file is being saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\category_assignments.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using Copper Point of Sale (v3.06) by NCH Software. The inventory file is at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\inventory_items.csv and contains exactly 12 items. The categories to be created are Beverages, Snacks, Electronics, and Stationery. The expected assignments are: Beverages (Coffee Beans 1lb, Green Tea Box, Orange Juice 64oz), Snacks (Trail Mix Bag, Granola Bars 6pk, Potato Chips Family Size), Electronics (USB-C Cable 6ft, Wireless Mouse, Phone Charger 20W), and Stationery (Notebook College Ruled, Ballpoint Pen 10pk, Sticky Notes 3x3). The output file must be at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\category_assignments.txt with the format 'Item Name | Category Name' (one per line).
